title: Zero-Shot Long-Horizon Dexterous Manipulation via Multi-View 3D-Grounded VLM Reasoning
headline: Zero-Shot Dexterous Manipulation via Multi-View 3D-Grounded VLM Reasoning for Long-Horizon Tasks
tldr: We propose a zero-shot framework that grounds language instructions into 3D task plans using multi-view RGB images. By leveraging a vision-language model for 2D keypoint extraction and multi-view fusion, the system generates executable 3D plans without training a policy.
significance: This approach enables flexible, language-driven dexterous manipulation without costly policy training, opening new possibilities for autonomous robotics.

title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
headline: OmniAgent: Real-Time Interactive Video Understanding via Native Active Perception and Cross-Modal Analysis
tldr: OmniAgent introduces a native omni-modal agent that actively selects frames for processing, reducing computational cost while maintaining accuracy. By integrating cross-modal reasoning, it achieves efficient, real-time video understanding without global pre-scanning.
significance: This approach enables scalable, low-latency video analysis for applications ranging from surveillance to autonomous driving.

title: Quantum solitons and their quantum walks in transmon arrays
headline: Exploring Quantum Solitons and Their Walks in Coupled Transmon Arrays
tldr: The study models a linear chain of capacitively coupled transmons as an attractive Bose-Hubbard system, revealing discrete soliton states in the low-energy spectrum. It further analyzes how these solitons propagate as quantum walks, offering insights for quantum simulation with superconducting circuits.
significance: It demonstrates a new platform for simulating nonlinear quantum dynamics, bridging condensed-matter physics and quantum information processing.

title: Beyond the Current Observation: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Controllable Non-Markov Games
headline: RNG-Bench: A New Benchmark for LLMs in Hidden-State Non-Markov Games
tldr: RNG-Bench introduces a suite of controllable non-Markov games that require agents to reconstruct hidden states from partial observations. It enables evaluation of multimodal LLM policies beyond simple recall, providing a more realistic benchmark for closed-loop decision making.
significance: It provides a realistic, open-source benchmark that isolates hidden-state reconstruction, advancing the evaluation of multimodal LLMs in dynamic environments.

title: Constraints on Cosmic Strings from the Curl-Mode CMB Lensing Power Spectrum measured by ACT DR6
headline: ACT DR6 Curl-Mode Lensing Tightens Cosmic String Constraints Using Latest CMB Observations
tldr: The Atacama Cosmology Telescope’s sixth data release provides a curl‑mode lensing power spectrum that probes vector and tensor perturbations from cosmic strings. The analysis sets new upper limits on the string tension, tightening constraints by a factor of two compared to previous studies.
significance: This work demonstrates that CMB curl‑mode measurements can serve as a powerful, independent probe of topological defects in the early universe.

title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
headline: Simulating Human Users with Turing Rewards: A New LLM Benchmark
tldr: This paper introduces a method to train large language models to generate realistic user responses by optimizing a Turing reward, rather than matching a single ground truth. The approach improves user simulation for agent training, personalization evaluation, and social science research.
significance: It offers a more flexible and realistic benchmark for evaluating user simulation models, advancing AI assistants and personalization systems.

title: Fine-scale downflows above flare ribbons captured by Solar Orbiter/EUI
headline: Solar Orbiter Reveals Fine-Scale Downflows Above Flare Ribbons
tldr: Using the Extreme Ultraviolet Imager on Solar Orbiter, scientists captured tiny downflow structures—called riblets—above flare ribbons, offering new insight into energy transport during solar flares. These observations help refine models of magnetic reconnection and chromospheric heating.
significance: This discovery provides direct evidence of small-scale plasma dynamics that influence flare energy deposition, improving our understanding of solar flare mechanisms.

title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
headline: LOCUS: Building a Comprehensive Local Ordinance Corpus for U.S. Legal AI
tldr: The LOCUS project compiles machine-readable local ordinances across the United States, filling a critical gap in legal AI datasets. By aggregating zoning, housing, and public health codes, it enables more accurate AI-driven legal analysis and policy research.
significance: Providing a unified, authoritative source of local ordinances empowers AI models to understand and predict municipal regulations, a previously underrepresented domain.

title: Do as I Do: Dexterous Manipulation Data from Everyday Human Videos
headline: Leveraging Everyday Human Videos to Train Dexterous Robotic Manipulation
tldr: The paper presents a scalable approach to generate training data for dexterous robotic hands by extracting hand-object interactions from monocular RGB human videos. It introduces methods to overcome challenges in pose estimation and embodiment gaps, enabling robots to learn from abundant everyday footage.
significance: This work bridges the gap between human demonstration and robotic execution, opening avenues for cost-effective, large-scale manipulation learning.

title: Sparse-dense flight copy-based interactive mechanism to airline integrated with cruise speed control
headline: Integrated Flight Recovery and Rerouting Using Cruise Speed Control for Airlines
tldr: The paper proposes an integrated approach to flight recovery, aircraft rerouting, and passenger reallocation that preserves their interdependence. It incorporates cruise speed control to improve recovery performance.
significance: It offers a holistic, speed-optimized recovery framework that can reduce operational disruptions and costs.

title: Topological spectral form factor reveals emergent non-Hermitian single-particle $\mathcal{PT}$ transitions from many-body quantum chaos
headline: Spectral Form Factor Uncovers Non-Hermitian PT Transitions in Quantum Chaos
tldr: The study shows that the spectral form factor, a probe of quantum dynamics, can reveal emergent non-Hermitian PT symmetry breaking transitions at the single-particle level arising from many-body chaos. This provides a new non-perturbative tool linking topological defects in partition functions to universal chaotic behavior.
significance: It bridges quantum chaos and non-Hermitian physics, offering a novel diagnostic for phase transitions beyond local observables.

title: The Chandra-Gaia Catalog of Counterparts: Resolving ambiguous Gaia matches to X-ray sources in the Chandra Source Catalog using Machine Learning
headline: Machine Learning Resolves Ambiguous Gaia Matches to Chandra X-ray Sources
tldr: We develop a machine-learning framework that uses photometric and astrometric properties to cross-match Chandra X-ray sources with Gaia DR3 counterparts, outperforming spatial-only methods. The model identifies true matches, flags chance coincidences, and resolves multiple candidate ambiguities, providing a robust training set for future catalogs.
significance: This approach enhances the reliability of multi-wavelength astronomical catalogs, enabling more accurate studies of X-ray emitting objects.

title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
headline: Active Exploration Improves Sample Efficiency for Robust Preference-Based Reinforcement Learning
tldr: The paper proposes a model-based framework that actively guides exploration in preference-based RL, using pairwise behavior comparisons to learn reward models. This approach significantly boosts sample efficiency, especially in early learning stages, by jointly reasoning about exploration and preference signals.
significance: By actively directing exploration, the method addresses the sample inefficiency that hampers current passive preference-based RL, enabling faster and more reliable reward learning.

title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
headline: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation Enhances Reasoning in Large Language Models without Costly Chain-of-Thought Annotations
tldr: The paper introduces a self-distillation framework that conditions on rubric-based quality metrics, enabling large language models to learn reasoning skills without relying on expensive chain-of-thought annotations. Experiments show improved accuracy and robustness compared to traditional supervised distillation and reinforcement learning approaches.
significance: By eliminating the need for costly, noisy chain-of-thought data, this method offers a scalable path to more reliable reasoning in LLMs.

title: Superconductivity from interband coupling to ferroelectric quantum critical fluctuations in two dimensions
headline: Interband Coupling Drives Superconductivity Near 2D Ferroelectric Quantum Criticality
tldr: The study shows that transverse ferroelectric fluctuations, usually weakly coupled to electrons, can enhance superconductivity via interband interactions in two dimensions. This mechanism explains the observed superconducting dome around ferroelectric transitions in several materials.
significance: It resolves the long-standing puzzle of how ferroelectric criticality boosts superconductivity despite suppressed density–density coupling.

title: Future Dynamic 3D Reconstruction: A 3D World Model with Disentangled Ego-Motion
headline: Dynamic 3D Reconstruction: Disentangled Ego-Motion for Accurate Autonomous World Modeling
tldr: The paper introduces FR3D, a 3D world model that separates ego‑motion from environmental dynamics to improve physical consistency in long‑term predictions. By disentangling these components, FR3D reduces artifacts like morphing or vanishing objects, enabling more reliable forecasting for autonomous agents.
significance: FR3D advances autonomous navigation by providing a physically consistent, long‑horizon 3D world model that mitigates common generative model artifacts.

title: Unified Multimodal Autoregressive Modeling with Shared Context-Visual Tokenizer is Key to Unification
headline: Unified Multimodal Autoregressive Model Bridges Visual Tokenization Gap for Integrated Understanding and Generation
tldr: We present UniAR, a unified autoregressive framework that uses a single discrete visual tokenizer to unify multimodal understanding and generation. By eliminating separate visual tokenizers, UniAR achieves more coherent cross-modal representations and improved performance across vision-language tasks.
significance: UniAR demonstrates that a shared visual tokenizer can unify multimodal modeling, simplifying architecture and boosting cross-modal performance.

title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
headline: VERITAS: Real-Time Generator-Verifier Framework for Autonomous Robot Policy Steering and Self-Improvement
tldr: VERITAS introduces a generator-verifier architecture that lets pre-trained generalist robot policies steer themselves at inference time and learn from real-world feedback. By pairing a policy generator with a verifier that evaluates actions, robots can autonomously refine their behavior without external supervision.
significance: This approach enables robots to continuously improve performance in dynamic environments, reducing the need for costly retraining and expanding the applicability of generalist policies.

title: Variable-Width Transformers
headline: Variable-Width Transformers: Uneven Layer Capacity Boosts Language Model Efficiency and Performance
tldr: This study explores allocating variable width across transformer layers, showing that nonuniform capacity improves performance while reducing parameters. Empirical results suggest deeper layers benefit from wider widths, while shallow layers can be narrower without loss.
significance: It demonstrates that tailoring layer width can yield more efficient and powerful language models, challenging the conventional uniform design.

title: PAHSPECS: Spatially Resolved PAH Spectroscopy at cosmic noon with JWST MIRI MRS
headline: JWST MIRI MRS Reveals Spatially Resolved PAH Emission in z~1.1 Galaxies
tldr: Using JWST/MIRI MRS, the PAHSPECS program maps PAH features in star‑forming galaxies at cosmic noon. Forward modeling of data cubes with non‑parametric spatial distributions and PSF convolution recovers detailed PAH emission profiles.
significance: This work demonstrates a new technique for extracting spatially resolved PAH spectra from JWST data, advancing our understanding of galaxy evolution at z~1.

title: MOCHI: Motion Enhancement of Collaborative Human-object Interactions
headline: MOCHI: Enhancing Motion in Collaborative Human-Object Interactions for Real-Time Data Acquisition and Modeling
tldr: MOCHI presents a framework for capturing and enhancing motion data in collaborative human-object interactions, addressing the challenges of dynamic, multi-participant scenarios. By integrating high-fidelity sensing and continuous adjustment modeling, it enables more accurate anticipation and coordination among participants and shared objects.
significance: It advances the ability to model complex collaborative interactions, improving applications in robotics, AR, and human-robot teaming.

title: EventDrive: Event Cameras for Vision-Language Driving Intelligence
headline: Event Cameras Boost Vision‑Language Driving AI with Ultra‑Fast, High‑Dynamic Range Perception
tldr: Event cameras capture asynchronous brightness changes with microsecond latency, providing motion fidelity beyond frame‑based sensors. This enables robust perception in challenging driving scenarios like blur, glare, and rapid motion, enhancing vision‑language models for autonomous driving.
significance: By integrating event data, autonomous systems can achieve more reliable, real‑time understanding of dynamic environments, improving safety and performance.

title: EBench: Elemental Diagnosis of Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies
headline: EBench: A New Benchmark Diagnosing Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies Performance
tldr: EBench introduces a comprehensive simulation benchmark with 26 tasks across five capability dimensions and four generalization dimensions to evaluate generalist mobile manipulation models. The study reveals significant performance gaps among state-of-the-art models such as π0, XVLA, and InternVLA-A1.
significance: EBench provides a nuanced, multi-dimensional evaluation framework that moves beyond simple success rates, enabling deeper insights into the strengths and weaknesses of generalist manipulation policies.

title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
headline: ReproRepo: Scalable Reproducibility Audits via GitHub Issues
tldr: ReproRepo automates reproducibility audits by leveraging GitHub repository issues, reducing manual curation. It offers a scalable benchmark for evaluating LLM agents in reproducing research results.
significance: By streamlining reproducibility checks, ReproRepo accelerates scientific validation and fosters trust in computational research.

title: Sign-Rank, Index, and List Replicability: Connections and Separations
headline: New Lower Bounds on Sign Rank via Z2-Index and List Replicability
tldr: Researchers have derived new lower bounds on the sign rank of binary concept classes using the Z2-index and list replicability. These techniques provide tighter constraints for learning theory and help distinguish between different complexity measures.
significance: This work advances our understanding of the computational complexity of learning binary concepts, offering tools that could improve algorithm design and theoretical limits.

title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
headline: EvolveNav: Self‑Evolving Zero‑Shot Object‑Goal Navigation with Foundation Models
tldr: EvolveNav introduces a test‑time self‑learning framework that continuously updates its memory and priors while navigating to unseen objects. By leveraging foundation models, it reduces repeated errors and trial‑and‑error compared to static‑prior methods.
significance: It demonstrates that embodied agents can adapt on the fly, improving efficiency in zero‑shot navigation tasks.

title: Quartic Lyapunov functions for global fluid stability
headline: Quartic Lyapunov Functions Unlock Global Fluid Stability
tldr: The authors develop quartic Lyapunov functions that guarantee global stability for fluid flows. This approach overcomes the shortcomings of the traditional energy method, which cannot handle transient energy growth.
significance: It provides a more accurate and less conservative stability criterion for fluid dynamics.

title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
headline: Language Models Track Their Own Success Likelihood via a Hidden Value Axis
tldr: Researchers constructed a "value" axis for Qwen3-8B using synthetic reinforcement learning data, showing that activations along this axis correlate with verbalized confidence and rollout outcomes. The study demonstrates that language models internally encode the likelihood of achieving their goals.
significance: It reveals that LLMs possess an internal metric of goal alignment, informing future alignment and safety research.

title: T-Rex: Tactile-Reactive Dexterous Manipulation
headline: T-Rex: Advancing Dexterous Manipulation with Tactile-Responsive Vision-Language Models
tldr: T-Rex introduces a framework that integrates tactile feedback into Vision-Language-Action models, enabling robots to adaptively manipulate objects. By addressing data scarcity and evaluation gaps, it pushes robotic dexterity closer to human-level agility.
significance: It bridges a critical gap in robotic manipulation by embedding dynamic tactile perception into learning-based VLA systems.

title: Human Universal Grasping
headline: Human Universal Grasping: A Flow-Matching Model for Diverse Robot Grasps
tldr: Humans naturally grasp objects effortlessly, but robots lag behind. We present HUG, a flow-matching model that generates diverse human-like grasps from a single RGB-D image, enabling more general robot manipulation.
significance: By leveraging human grasp data, HUG bridges the gap between human dexterity and robotic manipulation, paving the way for more versatile and efficient robots.

title: BRDFusion: Physics Meets Generation for Urban Scene Inverse Rendering
headline: BRDFusion: Merging Physics and Generative Models for Accurate Urban Scene Inverse Rendering
tldr: BRDFusion combines physically-based rendering with diffusion models to reconstruct urban scenes from videos, achieving realistic lighting and geometric consistency. The method unifies reconstruction accuracy with generative realism, enabling applications in content creation and autonomous driving simulation.
significance: It bridges the gap between physics-driven fidelity and data-driven realism, offering a scalable solution for urban inverse rendering.

title: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Solving Linear Inverse Problems
headline: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Linear Inverse Problems Using Diffusion Models
tldr: This work introduces a method to compute the exact posterior score for linear inverse problems, enabling accurate sampling without relying on approximate denoiser steering. The approach leverages diffusion models' unconditional scores and integrates measurement constraints analytically.
significance: It provides a principled way to solve inverse problems with diffusion priors, improving reconstruction fidelity.

title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
headline: Bridging 3D Physical Reasoning into Robot Policies with Geometric Action Models
tldr: This work introduces a geometric action model that extends vision‑language‑action frameworks into 3D reasoning, enabling robots to interpret user commands while accounting for object, camera, and action interactions. By moving beyond 2D latent spaces, the model improves policy learning in complex physical environments.
significance: It advances robot autonomy by integrating spatial reasoning directly into policy learning, bridging a critical gap between perception and action.

title: Compact Spin-Charge Separated Neural Quantum States for Valence-Bond States
headline: Compact Spin-Charge Separated Neural Quantum States for Efficient Valence-Bond Wavefunctions
tldr: This study introduces a neural network architecture that captures the sign structure and constrained Hilbert space of valence-bond states by designing it at an exactly solvable point. The resulting compact spin-charge separated neural quantum states enable efficient representation and simulation of quantum many-body wavefunctions.
significance: It provides a principled way to embed physical constraints into neural quantum states, improving their expressiveness and computational efficiency.

title: Galaxy-cluster-stacked Fermi-LAT, part IV: $\sim70$ GeV WIMP annihilation lines
headline: Stacked Fermi-LAT Galaxy Clusters Tighten Constraints on 70 GeV WIMP Annihilation Lines
tldr: Researchers combined gamma-ray data from dozens of galaxy clusters to search for 70 GeV dark matter annihilation signatures. The analysis sets the most stringent limits yet on velocity-dependent WIMP models, ruling out large portions of parameter space.
significance: This study narrows the viable mass range for WIMP dark matter, guiding future indirect detection efforts.

title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
headline: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting Improves Online RL Fine‑Tuning of Vision‑Language Agents
tldr: We propose a hierarchical advantage weighting scheme that decomposes sparse episode outcomes into richer transition‑level signals for online RL fine‑tuning of vision‑language agents. This approach preserves distinct feedback modes and yields more efficient policy updates compared to scalar reward reductions.
significance: By enabling more informative supervision from sparse outcomes, the method accelerates convergence and improves performance of pretrained VLAs in real‑time applications.

title: First Experimental Limit on the Thermal Solar Neutrino Flux
headline: Scientists Set First Experimental Limit on Thermal Solar Neutrino Flux Using KATRIN Data
tldr: Researchers analyzed public KATRIN data to search for low-energy solar neutrinos, setting the first experimental bound on the thermal neutrino flux. This result opens a new window into stellar cooling mechanisms and the low-energy tail of the proton-proton cycle.
significance: The study provides the first direct experimental constraint on the elusive thermal solar neutrino flux, advancing our understanding of stellar energy loss processes.

title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
headline: New Benchmark Dataset for LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
tldr: We present MetaSyn, a 442-entry dataset covering the full retrieval, screening, and synthesis stages of meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio, enabling systematic evaluation of LLM agents. This benchmark provides ground truth across the entire pipeline, facilitating rigorous testing of scientific reasoning capabilities.
significance: MetaSyn fills a critical gap by offering end-to-end ground truth for evaluating LLM agents in systematic review tasks.

title: R2RDreamer: 3D-aware Data Augmentation for Spatially-generalized 2D Manipulation Policies
headline: R2RDreamer: 3D-Aware Augmentation Boosts Spatial Generalization of 2D Manipulation Policies
tldr: R2RDreamer uses simulated 3D scenes to generate diverse, realistic augmentations for 2D robot manipulation tasks, enabling policies to generalize across unseen object poses and camera views. The method demonstrates significant performance gains on benchmark manipulation datasets with only a handful of real demonstrations.
significance: This work bridges the gap between limited real-world data and robust spatial generalization, offering a scalable solution for deploying manipulation policies in varied environments.

title: Curvature--Radiation Geometries Across the Second CHIME/FRB Fast Radio Burst Population
headline: Curvature Radiation Models Explored in the Second CHIME/FRB Fast Radio Burst Catalog
tldr: Researchers analyzed fast radio burst spectra using three curvature‑radiation templates—point‑source, one‑dimensional bunch, and paired‑bunch cavity—across the second CHIME/FRB catalog. All models achieved reduced chi‑squared values near unity, indicating comparable fits to the data.
significance: The study demonstrates that multiple curvature‑radiation geometries can equally explain FRB spectral properties, guiding future theoretical and observational efforts.

title: Instruct-Particulate: Scaling Feed-Forward 3D Object Articulation with Kinematic Control
headline: Instruct-Particulate: Scaling 3D Object Articulation with Kinematic Control via Instructional Models
tldr: We present Instruct-Particulate, a model that reconstructs articulated 3D objects from meshes and target kinematic specifications. By leveraging instruction-based conditioning, it generalizes across diverse object categories with limited annotated data.
significance: This approach enables scalable, data‑efficient reconstruction of articulated 3D models for animation, gaming, and robotics.

title: Leptonic flavour transfer: a new window on flavour gauge symmetries
headline: New Leptonic Flavour Transfer Mechanisms Reveal Hidden Gauge Symmetries in Particle Physics
tldr: The study introduces novel non‑Abelian flavour gauge groups that induce flavour‑transfer processes resembling charged‑current interactions in the lepton sector. By exploring various flavour structures, the authors systematically identify observable signatures that could probe these hidden symmetries at low energies.
significance: This work opens a new avenue for testing flavour gauge symmetries through accessible low‑energy lepton processes, potentially guiding future experimental searches.

title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
headline: Analogical Retrieval Enhances Complex Reasoning in Large Language Models' Performance
tldr: The paper introduces a retrieval-augmented reinforcement fine-tuning method that selects analogical examples to guide reasoning. Experiments show improved performance on reasoning benchmarks compared to standard lexical retrieval.
significance: It demonstrates that analogy-based retrieval can substantially improve reasoning in LLMs, offering a new direction for knowledge grounding.

title: Combined Analysis of Lattice QCD and Experimental Data on the Pion Transition Form Factor
headline: Combining Lattice QCD and Experimental Data to Refine the Pion Transition Form Factor
tldr: This study demonstrates a method to merge lattice QCD calculations with experimental measurements, enhancing the precision of the pion transition form factor. The approach leverages complementary strengths of both datasets to improve predictions for the muon g‑2 hadronic light‑by‑light contribution.
significance: Accurate determination of the pion transition form factor is essential for reducing uncertainties in the muon anomalous magnetic moment, a key test of the Standard Model.

title: Latent Spatial Memory for Video World Models
headline: Efficient Latent Spatial Memory Enhances 3D Consistency in Video World Models
tldr: This paper proposes a latent spatial memory that replaces explicit RGB point clouds, reducing computational cost and preserving richer latent features. The approach maintains 3D spatial consistency across generated frames without repeated rendering or VAE encoding.
significance: By eliminating costly rendering loops, the method enables scalable, high-fidelity video generation with robust 3D coherence.

title: OmniGameArena: A Unified UE5 Benchmark for VLM Game Agents with Improvement Dynamics
headline: OmniGameArena: Unified Benchmark for Vision‑Language Game Agents with Dynamic Evaluation
tldr: OmniGameArena introduces a single protocol that evaluates commercial, open‑weight, and specialized VLM agents across multiple games. It reports progressive improvement metrics instead of single‑attempt scores, enabling fair comparison of heterogeneous agent classes.
significance: This benchmark standardizes evaluation, enabling researchers to compare diverse VLM agents on equal footing and track learning progress in interactive game environments.

title: TSseek: Regular Expression-Based Similarity Search for Distributed Time Series Datasets
headline: TSseek: Regex-Based Pattern Search for Distributed Time Series Data in Big Data
tldr: TSseek introduces a regex-based similarity search engine that allows users to query distributed time series datasets using flexible pattern expressions instead of full sequences. The system supports scalable, distributed processing and demonstrates significant speedups over traditional exact-match methods.
significance: By enabling pattern-based queries, TSseek expands the applicability of time series analysis to real-world scenarios where precise sequences are unavailable.

title: Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks
headline: Measuring How Much Data Language Models Need to Master Formal Tasks
tldr: This study introduces a causal framework to quantify the amount of task-specific data required for language models to learn formal language tasks. By using controlled experiments, it demonstrates that data frequency strongly predicts learnability, offering a benchmark for future multi-task learning research.
significance: It provides a rigorous, data-driven benchmark for assessing and improving the efficiency of large language models across diverse tasks.

title: Rethinking the Divergence Regularization in LLM RL
headline: Rethinking Divergence Regularization for Stable RL in Large Language Models
tldr: This paper revisits divergence regularization in RL for LLMs, highlighting issues with ratio-clipping in PPO and GRPO. It proposes a more principled trust-region approach that improves stability and performance.
significance: It offers a new perspective on stabilizing off-policy RL for LLMs, potentially enhancing alignment and safety.

title: Linear Ricci-Trace Deformations and Operational Equivalence in Rastall-Type Gravity
headline: Exploring Linear Ricci-Trace Deformations in Rastall-Type Gravity: Structural Classification and Parameter Mapping
tldr: We classify linear Ricci–trace deformations of Einstein’s equations, revealing a family of Rastall-type models with modified Ricci-to-scalar coupling. The analysis provides a parameter dictionary linking these deformations to observable gravitational dynamics.
significance: This work clarifies the theoretical landscape of modified gravity, offering a systematic framework for future phenomenological studies.

title: Satellite compaction pathways: environmental drivers shaping dwarf galaxy corpulence in the TNG50 simulation
headline: Environmental Drivers of Compact Dwarf Galaxies in the TNG50 Simulation
tldr: We investigate how environmental factors lead to compact dwarf galaxies in the TNG50 simulation, focusing on satellite compaction pathways. Our analysis reveals that tidal interactions and gas stripping are key drivers of dwarf galaxy corpulence.
significance: This study provides a comprehensive framework for understanding the physical processes that produce compact dwarf satellites, informing models of galaxy evolution.

title: PTL-Diffusion: Manifold-Aware Diffusion with Periodic Terminal Laws
headline: PTL-Diffusion: Enhancing Diffusion Models with Manifold-Aware Periodic Terminal Laws
tldr: PTL-Diffusion introduces a manifold-aware diffusion framework that replaces the standard Gaussian terminal distribution with periodic terminal laws, enabling better modeling of data concentrated near low-dimensional manifolds. This approach improves generation quality for complex, structured datasets while maintaining analytical tractability.
significance: By aligning diffusion dynamics with underlying manifold structures, PTL-Diffusion offers a principled way to generate high-fidelity samples for data with rich geometric or semantic variations.

title: iMaC: Translating Actions into Motion and Contact Images for Embodied World Models
headline: iMaC: Bridging Actions to Motion Images for Robust Embodied AI
tldr: iMaC introduces a novel framework that translates low-dimensional action vectors into rich motion and contact images, enabling embodied world models to learn from visual dynamics rather than abstract parameters. The method demonstrates improved generalization across diverse robot embodiments and provides a benchmark dataset for future research.
significance: By converting actions into visual representations, iMaC unlocks more expressive, transferable, and physically grounded learning for embodied AI systems.

title: Non-Abelian braiding in Abelian Fractional Quantum Hall Phases from realistic interactions
headline: Realizing Non-Abelian Braiding in a Laughlin 1/3 Quantum Hall State
tldr: The study demonstrates that realistic two‑body interactions within the lowest Landau level can produce non‑Abelian braiding of quasiholes in the ν=1/3 Laughlin phase. Low‑energy excitations are found to lie almost entirely in the null space of the Moore‑Read three‑body Hamiltonian.
significance: It bridges the gap between Abelian and non‑Abelian quantum Hall physics, offering a realistic route to topological quantum computation.

title: Counterintuitive problems in discrete probability
headline: Counterintuitive Discrete Probability Problems and Solutions for LLM Benchmarking and Analysis of Common Errors
tldr: This manuscript presents a curated set of counterintuitive discrete probability puzzles, each accompanied by detailed solutions. The dataset serves as a benchmark to evaluate how well large language models reason through probabilistic problems and to identify systematic errors they may make.
significance: It provides a rigorous testbed for assessing and improving the probabilistic reasoning capabilities of modern LLMs.

title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
headline: Assessing LLM Accuracy in Dice Probability Tasks: A Benchmark Study
tldr: We benchmark eight state-of-the-art LLMs on standard and counterintuitive dice probability problems, testing with and without chain-of-thought prompting. Results show that while LLMs perform well on standard tasks, they struggle with counterintuitive scenarios, highlighting limitations in probabilistic reasoning.
significance: The study reveals critical gaps in LLMs' probabilistic reasoning, informing future model design and prompting strategies.

title: UniSHARP: Universal Sharp Monocular View Synthesis
headline: UniSHARP: Extending SHARP for Universal Monocular View Synthesis Across Camera Systems
tldr: UniSHARP generalizes the SHARP view synthesis framework to support a wide range of camera models, from perspective to fisheye and omnidirectional. By aligning images in a unified omnidirectional latent space, it enables photorealistic rendering across diverse viewpoints without pinhole-specific constraints.
significance: This work provides a versatile foundation for realistic monocular rendering in varied imaging contexts, paving the way for more flexible visual synthesis applications.

title: Agentopia: Long-Term Life Simulation and Learning in Agent Societies
headline: Agentopia: Simulating Long-Term Social Learning with LLM-Powered Agent Societies for Human Behavior
tldr: Agentopia explores whether large language models can learn from extended simulated social interactions, extending beyond the typical day-scale simulations. By modeling long-term agent societies, it aims to improve LLMs’ understanding of human behavior.
significance: It bridges the gap between short-term agent simulations and realistic human social learning, potentially enhancing AI’s social cognition.

title: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Graph Memory and Agentic Retrieval Mechanism
headline: MemDreamer: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Long Video Understanding via Hierarchical Memory
tldr: MemDreamer introduces a plug‑in framework that separates perception from reasoning, allowing incremental streaming of long videos into a hierarchical graph memory. An agentic retrieval mechanism then queries this memory, enabling efficient, scalable understanding without token explosion.
significance: This approach enables scalable, real‑time analysis of hours‑long videos, overcoming the limitations of current vision‑language models.

title: GJ 3929 b as the First Complete Rocky Worlds DDT Data Set
headline: GJ 3929 b: First Complete Rocky Worlds Dataset Reveals Atmosphere Search via JWST
tldr: The Rocky Worlds DDT survey has produced the first complete data set for GJ 3929 b, a low‑temperature rocky exoplanet orbiting an M dwarf. By probing day‑night heat redistribution and CO₂ absorption with JWST, the study seeks to determine whether such planets can sustain atmospheres.
significance: This dataset marks a pivotal step toward understanding atmospheric retention on rocky worlds around the most common stars in the galaxy.
